description: Invalid NPI — fails Luhn check digit (short-circuits the rule engine)
Invalid NPI — wrong format, not 10 digits (short-circuits the rule engine)
NCCI conflict only — basic metabolic panel billed with comprehensive panel
MUE limit issue only — comprehensive metabolic panel reported above its unit limit

Missing modifier 25 only — problem E/M in a preventive-visit context, no modifier 25. Uses Z00.01 (not Z00.00) so the diagnosis-to-procedure rule, which matches only Z00.00 exactly, does not also fire.

Diagnosis-to-procedure mismatch only — routine exam dx billed with problem E/M. Modifier 25 is present so the modifier rule does not also fire (it only checks modifier absence; the dx-conflict rule ignores modifiers entirely).
Multiple rule-layer findings in one claim — NCCI bundling, MUE overage, dx-procedure mismatch, and missing modifier 25 all at once
Clean claim — preventive visit code correctly paired with well-visit diagnosis, no findings expected
Clean claim — problem-oriented E/M with a matching acute diagnosis, no findings expected

Medicare coverage concern — HbA1c billed against a routine-exam diagnosis rather than a diabetes-related one. Coverage Agent finding only; requires --live evaluation (real LLM call) to actually surface this label, since offline mode mocks the Coverage Agent.

Coding defensibility concern — high-level E/M supported only by an unspecified symptom diagnosis. Coding Agent finding only; requires --live evaluation to surface this label, since offline mode mocks the Coding Agent.
Mixed — rule-layer NCCI conflict alongside a Medicare coverage concern (agent-layer label requires --live evaluation)
Mixed — rule-layer missing modifier 25 alongside a coding defensibility concern (agent-layer label requires --live evaluation)
Kitchen sink — every rule-layer finding plus both agent-layer findings in one claim (agent-layer labels require --live evaluation)
